<!-- =========================================================================
     TABLES, MATH & CODE
     ========================================================================= -->

## Simple table

A basic pipe table.
Use `:---`, `:--:`, `---:` on the separator row to left-,
center-, or right-align a column.

  | Component   | Class          | Where defined          |
  | :---------- | :------------- | :--------------------- |
  | Prompt box  | `.prompt-box`  | styles.css / style.typ |
  | Data card   | `.custom-card` | styles.css / style.typ |
  | Inline code | `.inline-code` | inline.lua             |

  : A simple, auto-width table.
    {#tbl-simple}

## Complex table

Fine-grained control: set relative column widths with a `{tbl-colwidths="[...]"}`
attribute on the caption, put math (`$\rho$`) in a header, and color individual
cells with an inline span, e.g. `[0.87]{style="color: green; font-weight: bold;"}`.

  | Run               | Config                         | Compute      | Score                                            | $\rho$                                           |
  | :---------------- | :----------------------------- | :----------- | :----------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------- |
  | **`baseline`**    | LR-6e-4 BS-8192                | 1× GPU       | 0.72                                             | 0.64                                             |
  | **`optimized`**   | LR-1.7e-3 BS-8192 + warmup     | 2× GPU       | [0.87]{style="color: green; font-weight: bold;"} | [0.75]{style="color: green; font-weight: bold;"} |
  | **`distributed`** | As above + gradient checkpoint | 2× GPU + CPU | 0.85                                             | 0.74                                             |

  : A wider table with custom column widths, math headers, and colored cells.
    {#tbl-complex tbl-colwidths="[22, 34, 16, 14, 14]"}

## Math

Inline math renders with `$...$`, e.g. the temperature $\tau$ scales the logits.
Display math uses `$$...$$` and is centered and enlarged in both formats.
You can control the size further with `.math-display` and `.math-inline`:

```{=html}
<div style="margin-bottom: 2em;"></div>
```
```{=typst}
#v(2em)
```

$$
  L = -\frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \log \frac{e^{\text{sim}(a_i, p_i) / \tau}}{\sum_{j=1}^{n} e^{\text{sim}(a_i, p_j) / \tau}}
$$

```{=html}
<div style="margin-top: 2em;"></div>
```
```{=typst}
#v(2em)
```

```{=typst}
#pagebreak()
```

## Code blocks

Fenced code blocks get a boxed, theme-aware background (light in light mode, a dark
Dracula palette in dark mode --- configured via `highlight-style` in `index.qmd`).
Name the language after the backticks for syntax highlighting.

To fine-tune appearance (padding, corner radius, font size, colors), edit the
`#show raw` rules in `layout.typ` for the PDF and the `--code-block-*` variables
plus the *"Code blocks & highlighting"* section of `styles.css` for the web.

```Dockerfile
FROM base AS builder

# Install uv
COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /bin/

# Hatchling requires a version number to package / install with the code. 
# Pass via --build-arg
ARG VERSION=0.0.0

# Install dependencies into .venv
# Because of --no-install-project, this dependency layer will only invalidate 
# when we install new dependencies, not change our source code
COPY pyproject.toml uv.lock ./
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=uv_username \
    --mount=type=secret,id=uv_password \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv \
    UV_INDEX_X_USERNAME=$(cat /run/secrets/uv_username) \
    UV_INDEX_X_PASSWORD=$(cat /run/secrets/uv_password) \
    uv sync --frozen --no-dev --group training --no-install-project

# Install our project code
COPY README.md LICENSE.md ./
COPY ./scripts ./scripts

# `--no-editable`: installs our project dependency straight into `site-packages` 
# = no messing around with env variables to find the package
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=uv_username \
    --mount=type=secret,id=uv_password \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv \
    UV_INDEX_X_USERNAME=$(cat /run/secrets/uv_username) \
    UV_INDEX_X_PASSWORD=$(cat /run/secrets/uv_password) \
    uv sync --frozen --no-dev --group training --no-editable


# ---- STAGE 3: Production --------------------------------------------------------------
FROM base AS production

WORKDIR /app

# Just need the environment and the source code!
COPY --link --from=builder /app/.venv /app/.venv
COPY --link --from=builder /app/scripts /app/scripts

# Need to specify this placeholder for the runtime call!
ENV CMD=""
CMD exec $CMD
```
